title: cerasus × sacra
intro: Cerasus × sacra, also known as the Japanese apricot or Japanese plum, is a hybrid plant between the Prunus mume and Prunus salicina species, resulting in a unique fruit-bearing tree with a blend of characteristics from both parent plants. The bark, leaves, and flowers of the Cerasus × sacra plant are used in traditional medicine, particularly in Korean and Chinese herbalism, to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Cerasus × sacra is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusement, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish in various dishes.
